caption:
OpenAI and Broadcom have unveiled the "Jalapeño" chip, designed specifically for large language model inference. Expected to launch at scale by late 2026, this custom hardware marks OpenAI's first foray into specialized silicon after years of model development. The collaborative effort aims to enhance efficiency and performance in AI applications.

How do you think custom chips will impact AI development?

Sources: The Decoder, OpenAI

#OpenAI #Broadcom #AI #TechNews #Jalapeño #CustomHardware #Silicon #Innovation #LLM #MachineLearning #AIResearch #FutureTech

📷 Cover photo: 首相官邸ホームページ · CC BY 4.0

slides.headline: OPENAI JUST UNVEILED A CUSTOM CHIP CALLED "JALAPEÑO"
slides.sub: Here's what you need to know.
slides.image_prompt: A close-up of a sleek, modern chip on a workbench, with engineers discussing in the background.

slides.headline: "JALAPEÑO" IS TAILORED FOR LARGE LANGUAGE MODEL INFERENCE
slides.sub: It was co-developed with Broadcom and designed from scratch.
slides.image_prompt: Engineers examining a silicon wafer in a high-tech lab, with focused expressions.

slides.headline: DESIGN TO TAPE-OUT TOOK JUST NINE MONTHS
slides.sub: This is claimed to be the fastest ASIC development cycle known.
slides.image_prompt: A team of engineers in a meeting room, with blueprints and digital schematics displayed on screens.

slides.headline: DEPLOYMENT PLANNED FOR LATE 2026 AT GIGAWATT SCALE
slides.sub: This will be a significant step for OpenAI's hardware ambitions.
slides.image_prompt: A futuristic server room with rows of high-performance computing hardware and glowing lights.

slides.headline: THIS CHIP COULD ENHANCE AI PERFORMANCE AND EFFICIENCY
slides.sub: Follow @everydayhypehq for daily AI + Tech breakthroughs.
slides.image_prompt: A conceptual image of AI technology integrating seamlessly into everyday life, with diverse people interacting with devices.

story.title: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil "Jalapeño," a custom chip built for LLM inference
